Code fragment: public final TableField<RetiredUserStatsRecord, Integer> RETIRED_USER_ID = createField(DSL.name("retired_user_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key 'retired_user_id' even though there's already a unique constraint on 'user_id' (RETIRED_USER_STATS_USER_ID_KEY) which could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using 'user_id' as the primary key instead of introducing a surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<SystemUsersRecord, String[]> ROLES = createField(DSL.name("roles"), SQLDataType.CLOB.array().defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("'{}'::text[]"), SQLDataType.CLOB.array())), this, "");
Explanation: The ROLES column has a default value of an empty array but is marked as NULLABLE, which means it can never be NULL in practice since new records will get the default empty array. Consider making this column NOT NULL since it has a meaningful default value.

Code fragment: public final TableField<TeamsRecord, Integer> TEAM_ID = createField(DSL.name("team_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (TEAM_ID) even though TEAM_NAME has a unique constraint and could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using TEAM_NAME as the primary key if team names are stable and immutable, or rename TEAM_ID to something more descriptive like TEAMS_ID.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserChangesRecord, LocalDateTime> CREATED_UTC_TIMESTAMP = createField(DSL.name("created_utc_timestamp"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(6), this, "");
Explanation: The CREATED_UTC_TIMESTAMP column is marked as NULLABLE but likely should never be NULL since it represents when the record was created. Consider adding a NOT NULL constraint with a default value of the current timestamp, or mark it as nullable(false).

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserChangesRecord, LocalDateTime> UPDATED_UTC_TIMESTAMP = createField(DSL.name("updated_utc_timestamp"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(6), this, "");

Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, Boolean> IS_CAPTAIN = createField(DSL.name("is_captain"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("false"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN)), this, "");
Explanation: The column 'is_captain' has a default value of 'false' but is marked as NULLABLE, meaning it can never be NULL in practice since new rows will get the default value. Consider making this column NOT NULL to accurately reflect its intended usage.
